Hesse_cut_dependency_geometry.interpretation: The 39 Hesse pencils define a [39,4,36] dependency code: no three dependency columns are collinear, no four are coplanar, and every 36-sector complement of a 3-sector plane is a circuit.

Hessian_monodromy_signature.interpretation: The closure matrix attached to H(H(X_i)) has a projective eigenratio. Split pencils have orders in F_p^*, nonsplit pencils have norm-one orders in F_{p^2}; the order spectrum is the Hessian-monodromy signature.

character_basis_orthogonality_refinement.interpretation: The v24 A42+secondary basis has already reached the primitive idempotent basis: the character transform is a permutation/zero-one basis with determinant 1.

main_conclusion.Hessian_monodromy: sectorwise projective eigenratio orders refine the sector data but do not alone separate all sectors
main_conclusion.new_intrinsic_invariant: the 39 Hesse pencils span a 35-dimensional Hesse cut with a 4-dimensional dependency code in uniform projective position
main_conclusion.pseudomodular_basis: A42+secondary has already diagonalized to the primitive point basis; the pseudomodular Verlinde algebra is split F_p^39

verified_claims: The 39 Plucker/Hesse pencil vectors have rank 35; hence there are exactly four independent linear dependencies.
The dependency code is length 39 and dimension 4, with no zero or repeated projective columns.
Every line in the dependency PG(3) contains exactly two sector columns and every plane contains exactly three sector columns.
Therefore the dependency configuration is in uniform projective position; the minimum row circuit weight is 36.
The Hessian closure pairs yield a sectorwise monodromy signature with five distinct projective eigenratio orders.
q42 plus Hessian discriminant separates all 39 sectors; q42 plus Hessian order separates 38.
The A42+secondary character basis is already a zero-one primitive basis with determinant 1, so the final pseudomodular algebra is the split point algebra without hidden multiplicity.
